title: euphorbia polygalifolia proles mariolensis
intro: Euphorbia polygalifolia proles mariolensis, also known as the Galician spurge, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Iberian Peninsula. It belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family and is characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and milky sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. Medicinally, it is valued for its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in topical preparations for wounds and skin conditions, and it can be prepared as an infusion, ointment, or tincture.
